600.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Ka'b b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Mabdhul b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Mazin b. al-Najjar
600.spouses: Zughayba bint Aws of B.Mazin B.al-Najjar

601.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman bin Ka'b b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Mabdhul b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Mazin b. Najjar Ansari
601.arabic: أبو ليلى المازني ,عبد الرحمن بن كعب

602.full_name: Zaid bin Asim bin 'Amr b. 'Auf  b. Mabdhul b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Mazin b. al-Najjar

603.full_name: Ghaziyah bin 'Amr b. 'Atiyah b. Khanasa b. Mabdhul b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Mazin b. al-Najjar

604.full_name: Habib bin Zaid bin Asim bin 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Mabdhul b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Mazin b. al-Najjar
604.bio: It was as though he wanted to celebrate his Islam until the very last moment of his life. Now, if Habib, on that day, had tried to escape this horrible death by a pretense of his faith in Musailamah's prophethood, his faith would not have been questioned, doubted or blemished in any way. But he was a man who had witnessed the Second Pledge of Al-'Aqabah along with his father, mother, brother, and aunt, and ever since those decisive blessed moments he had carried upon his shoulders the responsibility that ensued his oath and faith to the fullest. He could not for a moment hold his life and principles as separable. Therefore, he found a rare opportunity to win his life once and for all. His life was an embodiment of his faith. It embodied his stead-fastness, greatness, heroism, sacrifice, and martyrdom for the sake of Right and Truth, the splendor of which surpassed all victories.

605.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Zayd bin 'Asim b. Ka'b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. Mabdhul b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Mazin b. al-Najjar

611.full_name: 'Auf bin Harith bin Rifa'a b. al-Harith b. Sawad b. Malik b. Ghanm b. Malik b. al-Najjar
611.bio: A member of Khazraj tribe of Madinah.  Met with the prophet (ﷺ)in the elevent year of his mission, 620 CE.  Among the six people from Yathrib to make the pledge to accept and spread Islam and to return the following year with new converts. According to some sources, he was involved in killing Abu Jahl with his brother Mu'awwidh but most authentic story is that it was Mua'dh bin 'Amr.

612.full_name: Mu'adh bin Harith bin Rifa'a b. al-Harith b. Sawad b. Malik b. Ghanm b. Malik b. al-Najjar
612.arabic: معاذ بن عفراء/ معاذ بن الحارث بن رفاعة

613.full_name: Mu'awwidh bin Harith bin Rifa'a b. al-Harith b. Sawad b. Malik b. Ghanm b. Malik b. al-Najjar
613.bio: A member of Khazraj tribe of Madinah.  He was involved in killing Abu Jahl at Badr, with Mua'dh bin 'Amr.

668.arabic: عبد الله بن طارق بن عمرو
668.mother_name: A woman from B.'Udhra of B.Kahil
668.bio: He was taken to Makkah after the event of al-Raj'i. But he broke loose and drew his sword. The men stoned him until his death.

669.full_name: Mu'attib bin 'Ubaid b. Iyyas b. Tamim b. Sha'ba b. Sa'dallah b. Faran b. Bali b.al-Haaf
669.mother_name: A woman from B.'Udhra of B.Kahil
669.bio: According to some sources, He was from B.Zafar ( معتب بن عبيد بن سواد بن الهيثم بن ظفر). He may not be killed on day of al-Raji' according to most sources.
